title: Inga arrabidae
taxonomy: Inga arrabidae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. The genus Inga is a part of this family and comprises over 300 species, primarily found in tropical regions. Inga arrabidae, like other members of the genus, is a tree or shrub with compound leaves and produces small, fragrant flowers. The plant is native to Central and South America, where it grows in tropical rainforests and other humid environments. Inga arrabidae is also known to be a host plant for certain species of butterflies and moths.

intro: Inga edulis, also known as the "ice cream bean tree", is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. The bioactive constituents of Inga edulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Inga edulis is commonly used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues including digestive problems and fever.
